rationale: The cited paper is used in the theory-building section to support a core premise of the article: process and outcome information are represented at different abstraction levels (concrete vs. abstract). The citation also appears in the design rationale as prior precedent for contrasting experiences with positive outcome/negative process versus positive process/negative outcome. Although grouped with other references, it plays a clear framing and setup role beyond a perfunctory mention.
relation_description: The citing paper treats Zhao et al. (2007) as part of the conceptual foundation for linking process-versus-outcome focus to construal level in experience evaluation. It also uses the paper as methodological precedent for the type-of-experience contrast used in its studies.
